name: France
facts: France is the most visited country in the world by international tourists.
Paris is called the City of Light.
didYouKnow: The Louvre museum in Paris is the world's most visited museum.
hintTokens: Western Europe country with Paris as capital.
mnemonic: Think: F in France, F in Eiffel.
learningObjective: Notice France on the Atlantic side of Western Europe.

name: Germany
facts: Germany has more than 16 federal states.
The Rhine River is one of its key waterways.
didYouKnow: Germany is famous for the Black Forest region.
hintTokens: Central Europe country east of France.
mnemonic: Germany sits in the center of Europe.
learningObjective: Find Germany between France and Poland.

name: Taj Mahal
facts: UNESCO World Heritage Site in Agra.

name: Eastern Ghats
hintTokens: Discontinuous range on east coast side

name: Satpura Range
hintTokens: Central India range south of Narmada

name: Vindhya Range
hintTokens: Central India range north of Narmada

name: Western Ghats
hintTokens: Runs parallel to India west coast

name: Brahmaputra
facts: Known for massive seasonal flow and flooding plains.

name: Ganges
facts: One of the most sacred rivers in India.

name: Acropolis of Athens
facts: Ancient citadel above Athens.
Home to the Parthenon and other classical structures.
didYouKnow: It is one of the most important symbols of ancient Greece.
learningObjective: Locate Acropolis of Athens in Greece and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Alhambra
facts: Palace and fortress complex in Granada.
Famous for Islamic architecture and courtyards.
didYouKnow: Its name comes from Arabic for “the red one.”
learningObjective: Locate Alhambra in Spain and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Angkor Wat
facts: Largest religious monument in the world by land area.
Originally built as a Hindu temple.
didYouKnow: It later became a major Buddhist temple complex.
learningObjective: Locate Angkor Wat in Cambodia and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Big Ben
facts: Nickname for the Great Bell at Elizabeth Tower.
Located at the Palace of Westminster.
didYouKnow: People often use Big Ben to refer to the entire clock tower.
hintTokens: This landmark is in United Kingdom.
learningObjective: Locate Big Ben in United Kingdom and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Burj Khalifa
facts: Tallest building in the world.
Located in Dubai.
didYouKnow: It has over 160 floors.
hintTokens: This landmark is in United Arab Emirates.
learningObjective: Locate Burj Khalifa in United Arab Emirates and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Chichen Itza
facts: Major Maya archaeological site in Yucatán.
Known for the stepped pyramid El Castillo.
didYouKnow: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.
learningObjective: Locate Chichen Itza in Mexico and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Christ the Redeemer
facts: A giant Art Deco statue overlooking Rio de Janeiro.
It stands atop Corcovado mountain.
didYouKnow: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.
mnemonic: Rio's giant statue = Christ the Redeemer.
learningObjective: Locate Christ the Redeemer in Brazil and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Colosseum
facts: A large amphitheater in Rome.
Used for public spectacles in ancient times.
didYouKnow: It could hold tens of thousands of spectators.
learningObjective: Locate Colosseum in Italy and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Eiffel Tower
facts: Built for the 1889 World's Fair in Paris.
It was once the tallest man-made structure in the world.
didYouKnow: It was criticized at first, but later became a symbol of France.
learningObjective: Locate Eiffel Tower in France and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Mount Everest
facts: Highest mountain above sea level.
Located in the Himalayas.
didYouKnow: Its height is 8,848.86 meters.
learningObjective: Locate Mount Everest in Nepal/China and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Forbidden City
facts: Imperial palace complex in Beijing.
Home of Chinese emperors for centuries.
didYouKnow: It has nearly 1,000 surviving buildings.
learningObjective: Locate Forbidden City in China and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Golden Gate Bridge
facts: A suspension bridge at the entrance to San Francisco Bay.
Known for its International Orange color.
didYouKnow: It was the longest suspension bridge span when completed.
hintTokens: Famous orange bridge in San Francisco.
This landmark is in United States.
learningObjective: Locate Golden Gate Bridge in United States and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Grand Canyon
facts: A steep-sided canyon carved by the Colorado River.
Located in Arizona.
didYouKnow: Its layered rocks reveal millions of years of Earth history.
hintTokens: This landmark is in United States.
mnemonic: Grand = giant canyon in U.S. Southwest.
learningObjective: Locate Grand Canyon in United States and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Great Wall of China
facts: A network of fortifications built across northern China.
Many sections were built and rebuilt over centuries.
didYouKnow: The wall is not one single continuous structure.
hintTokens: Long historic wall in northern China.
learningObjective: Locate Great Wall of China in China and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
facts: Highest mountain in Africa.
A freestanding volcanic mountain.
didYouKnow: It has three volcanic cones: Kibo, Mawenzi, and Shira.
learningObjective: Locate Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Machu Picchu
facts: An Inca citadel in the Andes Mountains.
It sits high above the Urubamba Valley.
didYouKnow: It remained largely unknown internationally until the early 1900s.
learningObjective: Locate Machu Picchu in Peru and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Moai of Easter Island
facts: Monolithic human figures carved by the Rapa Nui people.
Located on Easter Island in the Pacific.
didYouKnow: Many moai have bodies buried below ground level.
hintTokens: Stone head statues on Easter Island.
learningObjective: Locate Moai of Easter Island in Chile and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Mount Fuji
facts: Japan's highest mountain.
A near-symmetrical stratovolcano.
didYouKnow: It is an important cultural symbol in Japan.
learningObjective: Locate Mount Fuji in Japan and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Mount Rushmore
facts: Sculpture of four U.S. presidents carved into granite.
Located in South Dakota.
didYouKnow: Over 400 workers carved the monument from 1927 to 1941.
hintTokens: Four presidents carved in South Dakota.
This landmark is in United States.
learningObjective: Locate Mount Rushmore in United States and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Neuschwanstein Castle
facts: 19th-century palace in Bavaria.
Commissioned by King Ludwig II.
didYouKnow: It inspired Disney's fairy-tale castle style.
learningObjective: Locate Neuschwanstein Castle in Germany and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Niagara Falls
facts: A group of waterfalls on the Niagara River.
Straddles the border of Ontario and New York.
didYouKnow: It is a major source of hydroelectric power.
hintTokens: Border waterfall between Canada and U.S.
This landmark is in Canada/United States.
learningObjective: Locate Niagara Falls in Canada/United States and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Petra
facts: An archaeological city carved into rose-colored rock.
A major center of the Nabataean Kingdom.
didYouKnow: Petra is often called the Rose City.
learningObjective: Locate Petra in Jordan and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Pyramids of Giza
facts: Ancient pyramids near Cairo.
Built as royal tombs in ancient Egypt.
didYouKnow: The Great Pyramid was the tallest human-made structure for over 3,000 years.
learningObjective: Locate Pyramids of Giza in Egypt and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Sagrada Familia
facts: Large basilica in Barcelona designed by Antoni Gaudí.
Still under construction after more than a century.
didYouKnow: It combines Gothic and Art Nouveau forms.
learningObjective: Locate Sagrada Familia in Spain and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Statue of Liberty
facts: A gift from France to the United States.
It stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor.
didYouKnow: Its full name is Liberty Enlightening the World.
hintTokens: This landmark is in United States.
learningObjective: Locate Statue of Liberty in United States and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Stonehenge
facts: Prehistoric monument made of standing stones.
Located on Salisbury Plain in England.
didYouKnow: Some stones were transported from far distances.
hintTokens: This landmark is in United Kingdom.
learningObjective: Locate Stonehenge in United Kingdom and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Sydney Opera House
facts: A performing arts center with sail-like roof shells.
One of Australia's best-known landmarks.
didYouKnow: Its architect was Jørn Utzon of Denmark.
learningObjective: Locate Sydney Opera House in Australia and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Table Mountain
facts: Flat-topped mountain overlooking Cape Town.
A major symbol of South Africa.
didYouKnow: A “tablecloth” cloud sometimes forms over the top.
hintTokens: This landmark is in South Africa.
learningObjective: Locate Table Mountain in South Africa and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Taj Mahal
facts: A white marble mausoleum in Agra.
Built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan.
didYouKnow: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
learningObjective: Locate Taj Mahal in India and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Uluru
facts: Large sandstone monolith in central Australia.
Sacred to the Aṉangu people.
didYouKnow: It appears to change color at sunrise and sunset.
learningObjective: Locate Uluru in Australia and connect it with nearby regions.

name: Mount Everest
facts: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level.
It is in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border.
didYouKnow: Its summit is 8,848.86 meters above sea level.
learningObjective: Spot Everest in the Himalaya region north of India.

name: K2
facts: K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.
It lies in the Karakoram range.
didYouKnow: K2 is considered one of the toughest mountains to climb.
learningObjective: Differentiate K2 from Everest in South-Central Asia.

name: Andes
facts: The Andes are the longest continental mountain range.
They run along western South America.
didYouKnow: Aconcagua, the highest outside Asia, is in the Andes.
hintTokens: South American range along Pacific side.
mnemonic: Andes run down the west edge of South America.
learningObjective: Follow the north-south chain along western South America.

name: Himalayas
facts: The Himalayas contain the world's highest peaks.
They stretch across several countries in Asia.
didYouKnow: Many major Asian rivers begin near the Himalayas.
hintTokens: Mountain range north of India with Everest.
mnemonic: Himalayas = high mountains north of India.
learningObjective: Trace the long arc along northern India.

name: Amazon
facts: The Amazon has the largest drainage basin on Earth.
It carries more water than any other river.
didYouKnow: The Amazon rainforest is named after this river.
hintTokens: Huge South American river crossing Peru and Brazil.
mnemonic: Amazon = huge water in South America.
learningObjective: Locate the west-to-east path across northern South America.

name: Nile
facts: The Nile is one of the longest rivers in the world.
It flows north into the Mediterranean Sea.
didYouKnow: Ancient Egyptian civilization grew along the Nile.
hintTokens: Major African river flowing north through Egypt.
learningObjective: Trace the river from East Africa to Egypt.

name: California
facts: California is the most populous U.S. state.
It has both Pacific coast and high mountains.
didYouKnow: California's economy is one of the largest in the world if treated as a country.
hintTokens: U.S. west coast state with Los Angeles and San Francisco.
learningObjective: Find the long west-coast state on the Pacific.

name: Texas
facts: Texas is the second-largest U.S. state by area.
Its capital is Austin.
didYouKnow: Texas has its own power grid for much of the state.
hintTokens: Large southern U.S. state bordering Mexico.
mnemonic: Texas is the big one above Mexico.
learningObjective: Identify the large state above northeastern Mexico.
